page.content: In a comparative analysis of the AM group and ACT group, the data shows that for "No need for 2nd line treatment," the AM group's percentage is 89.3% (84.1 – 93.2) while the ACT group's percentage is 95% (88.8 – 98.3). For "T <37.5°C (**) at day 14," the AM group has 93.9% (89.3 - 96.7) and the ACT group has 97.0% (91.6 – 99.4). At day 28, the AM group reports 96.9% (93.5 – 98.9) for "T <37.5°C" compared to the ACT group's 99.0% (94.6 – 100.0).

page.content: Both groups report zero severe malaria cases in children over five years, while the AM group shows 1.9% (0.2 – 6.7) for children 0-5 years, the same as the ACT group at 1.9% (0.05 – 10.3). Neither group experienced coma or convulsions due to severe malaria, and adverse effects were reported as 14.2% (9.7 - 19.9)* in the AM group and 18.8% (11.7 - 27.8)* in the ACT group. Additionally, "New episode (day 15-28) (parasite positive)" was 12.8% (8.4 – 18.3) for the AM group and 9.9% (4.9 – 17.5) for the ACT group. For antimicrobial films, the control (plain LDPE films) aligns with the plain MC/HPMC, while for Nis (25000 IU/mL), it is consistent across all treatments: Nis (25000 IU/mL) + SD (0.25%), Nis (25000 IU/mL) + SB (0.1%), Nis (25000 IU/mL) + SL (0.3%), and Nis (25000 IU/mL) + PS (0.3%).
